moves.note: neutral/informal; invites the other person to share their view before any agreement/disagreement exchange

variants.ro: Ce crezi despre ce ți-am spus?
variants.en: What do you think about what I said?

moves.note: neutral/informal; seeking confirmation that the other person is on board
neutral/informal; checking whether the other person disagrees

variants.en: Do you have anything to object to?
Do you have something against the idea of . . .?

moves.note: informal; inviting the other person to suggest a way forward

variants.en: How do you want to proceed?

moves.note: neutral; flagging that what follows is a personal opinion
neutral; the core assent move in its basic forms

variants.en: Yes, it is. (contradicting a negative assumption)

variants.ro: da = sigur = desigur = așa este = este adevărat = de acord = am înțeles
variants.en: yes = sure = of course = that's right = that's true = agreed = understood

moves.note: neutral; enthusiastic or unambiguous assent
informal/formal variants; conceding the other person's position is correct
neutral; positive reaction to a suggestion
neutral; assent with a stated reservation or proviso

variants.ro: Da, atâta timp cât nu deranjezi vecinii.
variants.en: Yes, as long as you don't disturb the neighbours.

moves.note: informal/neutral; going along without enthusiasm

moves.move: Acknowledging and closing a practical arrangement
moves.note: neutral; agreeing on a specific plan, time, or next step

variants.en: Good, then it's fixed for Wednesday.

variants.ro: Bine, renunț la piscină pentru moment.
variants.en: OK, I'll give up the pool for now.

variants.ro: Bine, dar trebuie să ajungem repede și la florărie.
variants.en: OK, but we need to get to the florist's quickly too.

moves.note: informal/neutral; signalling you relate to what was said

variants.en: Anyone would do it like this.

moves.note: neutral; the core dissent move

variants.en: no = not at all = false = untrue

moves.move: Flat refusal / out of the question
moves.note: neutral/informal; emphatic rejection, stronger than simple disagreement

variants.en: It is out of the question.

moves.note: neutral/informal; hesitation or gentle challenge short of outright refusal

variants.ro: Nu cred că avem nevoie de...
Orice ați spune, nu mai ține cu mine.
variants.en: Whatever you say, I won't buy it any more.

moves.note: informal; de-escalating or withdrawing from a dispute
Reflex apology used to get attention, pass someone, or signal a minor interruption; no deep regret intended

variants.en: I hope you will excuse me!

moves.note: Speaker signals awareness they may be interrupting and invites permission to continue
The default expression of genuine sorry; neutral form fits most situations; elaborated forms add context

variants.ro: Îmi pare rău, nu am deloc bani mărunți pentru rest.
variants.en: I'm sorry, I haven't got any small change.

variants.ro: Îmi pare rău, dar așa stau lucrurile.
variants.en: I am sorry, but that is how it is.

variants.ro: Îmi pare rău, mamă, dar vremurile s-au schimbat.
variants.en: I am sorry, mum, but times have changed.

moves.note: Stronger register; cer scuze is standard formal; mii de scuze intensifies; Vă implor and Regretele mele are over-formal or dated

variants.en: I beg you to forgive me.

moves.note: Speaker accounts for being late or absent, often addressed to a teacher or group

variants.ro: Sper că ne-a fost iertată absența.
variants.en: I hope our absence has been forgiven.

moves.note: Speaker softens an imminent departure by hoping not to cause offence

variants.ro: Sper să nu vă supărați, dar trebuie să plec.
variants.en: I hope you won't mind, but I have to leave.

moves.move: Promise not to repeat the offence
moves.note: Follows an apology; commits speaker to future behaviour

variants.ro: Promit că nu se va mai repeta.
variants.en: I promise it won't happen again.

moves.note: Response moves: speaker waves off the apology
Said at the door when receiving guests; formal register via Poftiți

variants.en: Here you go! / Please come in!

moves.move: Inviting to the table / asking guests to sit
moves.note: Host directing guests to take their place at the table

variants.en: Come to the table! / Please sit down to eat!

moves.note: Informal proposal to go find food (e.g. on a train)

variants.ro: Mi-e foame. Hai la vagonul restaurant.
variants.en: I'm hungry. Let's go to the dining car.

moves.note: Standard meal-opening wish; said before eating
Raised-glass toast; Noroc is informal, Sănătate is neutral/universal

moves.move: Inviting to help oneself / offering food or drink
moves.note: Host encouraging guests to eat or try something

variants.ro: Vrei să încerci o pălincă din Ardeal?
variants.en: Would you like to try a pălincă from Ardeal?
You can't refuse something like that.

moves.note: Waiter or host indicating the guest's table or handing over menus

variants.en: Shall I bring you the menu as well?

moves.note: Guest ordering a drink; vă rog marks formal; bare noun phrase is neutral shorthand
Guest ordering courses or coffee; aș vrea / aș lua = polite conditional

variants.ro: Aș lua o cafea, dacă au espresso.
variants.en: I would like a coffee, if they have espresso.
I would like something sweet as well.
We would like two espresso coffees.

moves.note: Server moving through the courses
Guest signalling they cannot eat any more
Guest requesting the check at a restaurant
Giving payment, sometimes keeping the change

variants.en: Here's eight and I'll take them.

moves.note: Said after eating; Să vă fie de bine is a traditional host's response or blessing

variants.en: I hope you enjoyed it. / May it do you good!

moves.note: neutral; signals you are about to ask something

variants.en: I would like to ask something.

moves.move: Asking about someone's wellbeing / catching up
moves.note: informal/formal pair; the most common small-talk opener

variants.en: And you, how are you doing?

variants.ro: A propos, ce mai face prietena ta?
variants.en: By the way, how is your girlfriend?
How did you feel in England?
How is it at the dorm?

moves.note: informal/formal pairs; a dori is slightly more polite/tentative than a vrea

moves.move: Asking whether someone is in the mood for something
moves.note: informal; chef and poftă both convey appetite/inclination; poftă can take noun or verb
informal; chef used for stating or denying one's own inclination

variants.en: I feel like going for a walk.

variants.ro: Nu am chef de nimic azi.
variants.en: I don't feel like doing anything today.

moves.note: informal/formal pair; ce/de cine forms open the question to free choice
neutral; te/vă covers both informal and formal addressees in one form

moves.move: Expressing one's own interest or curiosity
moves.note: neutral; de + noun vs. să + verb distinction noted in en gloss

variants.en: It is not of interest to me.

moves.note: ranges from neutral to emphatic slang; mi-e perpendicular is playful/colloquial; mă doare-n cot is blunt idiom

variants.en: It is all the same to me.
It is all the same to me.
It is all the same to me.

moves.note: informal/neutral; seeks engagement or agreement from interlocutor

variants.ro: Vedeți și dumneavoastră ce multe informații găsim aici, nu?
variants.en: You can also see how much information we find here, can't you?

moves.note: neutral; typically used in group or semi-formal contexts

variants.ro: Așteptăm cu nerăbdare să vorbești despre pasiunea ta.
variants.en: We are eagerly waiting for you to talk about your passion.

moves.note: neutral; conversational opener suggesting prior acquaintance

variants.ro: Oare nu ne-am mai întâlnit undeva?

moves.note: informal; rhetorical question pushing interlocutor toward a choice
informal; signals shared context before a follow-up remark

variants.ro: Înțeleg că Iuliana are mulți invitați.
variants.en: I gather Iuliana has many guests.

variants.ro: Știu că tu iubești muntele și răcoarea. Ai deja un plan?
variants.en: I know you love the mountains and the cool air. Do you already have a plan?

moves.note: The core word alone or with a casual loanword; neutral to informal
Mulțumesc strengthened with mult / foarte mult / frumos; all neutral register

variants.en: Thank you very much! (lit. thank you beautifully)
Thank you very much for everything!

moves.move: Thanking for a specific thing (help, information, invitation, etc.)
moves.note: Mulțumesc + pentru + noun; register varies by context/addressee

variants.en: Thank you very much for the information.
Thank you very much for the information.
Thank you for the invitation to dinner!
Thank you very much for your understanding.

moves.note: Post-meal thanks; Sărut-mâna is a traditional/regional folk formula

variants.en: Thank you for the meal! (lit. I kiss your hand)

moves.move: Declining an offer politely (no thank you)
moves.note: Nu + mulțumesc/mulțumim to refuse while thanking; several set wordings

variants.en: No thank you, that is all.
No thank you, it's enough for the time being.
No, we've got enough bags, thank you.

moves.note: Mulțumesc embedded in a transaction, booking, or appointment context

variants.ro: Mulțumesc și eu. O zi bună!
variants.en: Thank you too. Have a good day!
Thank you, I'll come back tomorrow then.
Thank you, I'll pay for everything now.
That's fine, thank you very much.

moves.note: Formulaic replies to mulțumesc

variants.en: Think nothing of it. / Don't mention it.

moves.note: Stronger than mulțumesc; signals a real debt or favour
Single-word exclamations signalling approval or delight
Marking another person's achievement; formal form uses vă
Saying you are proud of the other person's achievement

variants.ro: Sunt mândru de tine/de voi/de dvs.

moves.note: Warmly noting that someone is doing well

variants.ro: Mă bucur să văd ce bine te descurci.
variants.en: I am glad to see how well you are doing!

moves.note: Responding to another person's loss or misfortune

variants.ro: Sunt alături de tine/de voi/de dvs.
Vă trimit cele mai sincere gânduri.

moves.move: Invoking a religious blessing as thanks
moves.note: Traditional/folk formula; rare in everyday urban speech

moves.move: State a like or preference (open template)
moves.note: neutral; these are frame/template forms meant to be completed with a noun, infinitive, or subjunctive clause

moves.move: State a like — specific example sentences
moves.note: neutral; concrete instances of Îmi place/plac with particular objects, usable as model sentences in drill

variants.ro: Îmi place acel stil clasic și mereu elegant.
variants.en: I like that classic and always elegant style.

variants.ro: Îmi place să merg pe jos.
Îmi plac pantofii italienești de piele.
Amândouă opțiunile îmi plac foarte mult.
variants.en: Both options appeal to me very much.

moves.move: Express that something pleases or interests one
moves.note: neutral; third-person verb constructions where the thing experienced is grammatical subject

variants.ro: Pe mine mă interesează foarte mult lecția despre oase.
variants.en: I am very interested in the lesson about bones.

variants.ro: Consider că e foarte relaxant să merg cu trenul.
variants.en: I think it's very relaxing to travel by train.

moves.move: Exclaim delight or enthusiasm (in the moment)
moves.note: informal–neutral exclamations; Ce fain / mișto / tare / ca lumea are informal / colloquial; Ce minunat / incredibil are neutral

moves.move: Express pleasure or craving in advance (eagerness / feel like)
moves.note: informal; speaker announces a desire or willingness to do something

variants.ro: Avem chef să ascultăm muzică rock.
variants.en: We feel like listening to rock music.

variants.ro: Ai poftă să mănânci o prăjitură cu ciocolată?
variants.en: Do you feel like eating a chocolate cake?

variants.ro: Aș vrea să-ți cumpăr un cadou de adio.
variants.en: I would like to buy you a farewell present.

moves.note: neutral–formal; used when agreeing to a request or offer
neutral; used to express that a past event, meal, or situation was enjoyable

variants.ro: Am mâncat ca la mama acasă.
variants.en: I ate like at my mother's home. (It was a home-cooked meal.)

variants.ro: Mi-a plăcut foarte mult cu tine.
variants.en: I have really enjoyed being with you.

moves.move: Express gladness or relief at a situation
moves.note: neutral–informal; speaker expresses pleasure that something has happened

variants.ro: Ce bine că azi avem o zi liberă!
variants.en: How great that we have a day off today!

variants.ro: Ce bine că ai venit la timp!
variants.en: How good that you arrived on time!

variants.ro: E minunat când poți îmbina utilul cu plăcutul!
variants.en: It's wonderful when you can combine the useful with the pleasurable!

moves.move: Make a pleasurable observation (general statement about enjoyable things)
moves.note: neutral; general assertions about what is pleasant, not tied to speaker's own preference

variants.ro: O cafea bună face minuni dimineața!
variants.en: A good coffee works wonders in the morning!

moves.move: Announce a decision driven by pleasure (narrative)
moves.note: informal; used in storytelling to mark a choice made out of enthusiasm — peripheral as a standalone drill item

variants.ro: Atunci m-am hotărât, vin și eu la aniversarea Iulianei.
variants.en: Then I've made up my mind — I'll come to Iuliana's birthday party too.

moves.note: Pronoun used when addressing one or more people informally
Intermediate pronouns between tu and dumneavoastră; dumneata is urban polite, mata is rural/familiar variant
Polite/formal you, singular and plural
Calling out to a group — children or generic friends
Terms of endearment used to call someone's attention or express warmth
Vocative forms used when calling out to one's mother or father
Vocative forms for grandmother and grandfather, including affectionate diminutives and regional variants
Vocative forms for extended family
Standard formal address using Domnul/Doamna/Domnișoara + last name; abbreviated forms (D-l, D-na, D-ra) used in writing
Vocative address using Domnule/Doamnă + professional title, with or without surname

moves.move: Address by title + professional role + surname (formal, full form)
moves.note: Full formal address combining title, profession, and name — used in official/bureaucratic contexts
Addressing someone by their institutional role rather than by name

moves.move: Address a mixed or general audience (formal)
moves.note: Opening address to a mixed audience in public or ceremonial speech
Reserved for royalty, clergy, and senior officials; highly niche in everyday A1–A2 use
Casual all-purpose greeting; interchangeable in everyday use
Neutral to formal; used at any register; 'Neața is the clipped informal form of Bună dimineața
Deferential or archaic; Să trăiți and Sărut mâna are older/regional; Salutări is stilted in speech
Attached to a greeting or used alone; faceți is the formal/plural form

variants.en: How are you? / What are you doing?

moves.note: Host greeting on arrival; ați venit is formal/plural, ai venit is informal singular

variants.ro: E o onoare să vă revedem!
variants.en: It is an honour for us to see you again.

variants.ro: Ne bucurăm să vă avem ca oaspeți!
variants.en: It is a pleasure for us to have you as our guests!

variants.ro: Ne face plăcere să vă revedem!
variants.en: It is a pleasure to see you again!

moves.note: Conventional response to Bine ați venit; v-am găsit is formal, te-am găsit is informal
Host gestures after the door or upon seating
Used at bedtime or as a late-evening parting
La revedere is the neutral/formal default; Pa is casual; Te pup is warm/affectionate; Du-te sănătos is a send-off
Future-oriented parting; Pe mâine and Pe diseară fix a specific time; Ne auzim / Mai vorbim imply a phone call

variants.en: Talk later! (lit. we'll hear each other)

moves.note: Closing good-wish, often added after goodbye; Numai bine is the most common
Used when receiving a guest or visitor
Conventional response when someone welcomes you

variants.en: Reply to a welcome (lit. I hope I have found you well)

moves.note: General congratulation on an achievement or good news

moves.move: Wish someone a happy birthday / long life
moves.note: Birthday and longevity toasts; 'Mulți ani trăiască!' is the standard birthday song formula

variants.en: Happy birthday! (lit. May you live many years!)

variants.ro: Abia aștept să cântăm cu toții: Mulți ani trăiască!
variants.en: I can't wait for us all to sing: Happy birthday (Many years of life)!

moves.note: Health wishes; used at toasts, after a sneeze, on parting, or as general well-wishing
General good-luck wishes before an endeavour

moves.move: Wish good luck with work or an exam
moves.note: Contextualised success wishes tied to a specific task

moves.move: Ask someone to keep fingers crossed
moves.note: Request for moral/superstitious support before an event

variants.en: Keep your fingers crossed for me!

moves.note: Daytime well-wishing on parting or mid-interaction
Bedtime parting wishes

moves.move: Wish a good journey / safe trip
moves.note: Farewell wishes for travellers
Wishes to a visitor or guest during their time somewhere

moves.move: Wish someone well in general / have a great time
moves.note: Broad well-wishing not tied to a specific activity

variants.ro: Să aveți parte numai de bine!
Să vi se împlinească toate dorințele!
variants.en: May all your wishes come true!
Hope it goes well for you!

moves.note: Niche farewell wish addressed specifically to a seller

moves.move: Wish well after a purchase (enjoy your new item)
moves.note: Said by a shopkeeper when the customer leaves

variants.en: I hope you enjoyed it. / May it do you good!
May it do you good! / You're welcome!

moves.note: Formal shop or service farewell inviting repeat custom

variants.ro: Așteptăm să reveniți cât mai curând!
variants.en: We look forward to seeing you again soon!

moves.move: Wish good luck for a newborn / new family member
moves.note: Wishes expressed about (not to) a newborn or new addition
Traditional wedding toast

variants.en: May you have a strong marriage!

moves.move: Offer God's blessing / say farewell with a blessing
moves.note: Religious parting formulas; somewhat traditional / regional

variants.ro: Domnul să aibă grijă de voi!

moves.note: Parting encouragement to look after oneself
Said to someone going through difficulty
Expression of sympathy on bereavement
neutral/informal; core identity question
neutral; for groups
neutral; used when picking someone out from a group

moves.move: Identify someone by name or role
moves.note: neutral; stating or confirming who a person is

variants.ro: Acela este un coleg din grupa doi.
variants.en: That is a classmate from group two.

variants.ro: Aceea este o colegă din Italia.
variants.en: That is a female classmate from Italy.

variants.ro: Ea este sora mea mai mică.

moves.note: informal/neutral; speaker asserts their own or another's true identity

variants.ro: Nu vorbești cu Ana acum! Maria sunt.
variants.en: You're not speaking with Ana now! It's Maria.
You don't even know who we are.

moves.note: neutral; third-person name inquiry
neutral; asking for a description of a person's nature
formal variants use vă/aveți; informal variant uses tu form

moves.move: State one's own profession, role, or nationality
moves.note: neutral; self-identification

variants.ro: Sunt student/studentă la facultatea de fizică.
variants.en: I am a student in physics.

variants.ro: Sunt elev/elevă la liceul de matematică.
variants.en: I am a pupil at the mathematics high school.
Yes. I am an Erasmus student.

moves.move: Ask where someone works or is employed
moves.note: formal; workplace/employer inquiry

variants.en: Which company do you work for?

moves.note: neutral; answering workplace questions

variants.ro: Lucrez la o firmă de telefonie.
variants.en: I work for a telephone company.

moves.note: formal; niche professional context

moves.move: Ask or confirm group membership / student status
moves.note: formal; checking whether interlocutors belong to a category

variants.en: They are not members of my family.

moves.note: neutral; possession question linked to identifying an owner

variants.ro: Ai cui sunt ochelarii de aici?
Ale cui sunt gențile de acolo?
variants.en: Whose are the bags over there?

moves.note: neutral; answering whose-questions

moves.move: Meta-vocabulary for the act of introducing
moves.note: Infinitive / dictionary forms — not utterances; peripheral reference items

variants.en: to introduce someone to someone else

moves.note: Speaker initiates the encounter before names are exchanged

variants.en: Let's get acquainted! / Let's introduce ourselves!

moves.note: Elicits the other person's identity; register varies by pronoun choice
Narrower than 'who are you' — targets the name specifically
Core self-identification utterances
Speaker presents someone else to the addressee

variants.en: Let me introduce my mother to you!

variants.ro: Vă prezint studenții de la engleză!
variants.en: Let me introduce the English language students to you!

variants.ro: Am onoarea să vi-l prezint pe Domnul director Popescu!
variants.en: I have the pleasure to introduce Mr Popescu, the manager!

variants.ro: Pe Richard îl cunoști? E prietenul nostru de la Londra.
variants.en: Do you know Richard? He's our friend from London.

moves.note: Nationality, occupation, marital status, reason for contact — the substance of self-introduction beyond a bare name

variants.en: I am a teacher of Romanian.

variants.ro: Sunt reporter la revista studențească „Ovidianum” și doresc să vorbesc cu studenți pasionați de istorie.
variants.en: I am a reporter for the student magazine 'Ovidianum' and I would like to speak with students passionate about history.

moves.note: Said by either party after names are exchanged; ranges from neutral to very formal

variants.en: Delighted to make your acquaintance. / Nice to meet you.
I am happy to meet you!

variants.ro: Îmi face plăcere să vă cunosc!
variants.en: It is a pleasure to meet you!

moves.note: Response to the pleasure-expression above; closes the exchange
neutral; used on or around 25 December
neutral; specific to the new year

moves.move: General celebration wish (birthday / name day / New Year)
moves.note: neutral; the all-purpose Romanian congratulatory phrase — covers birthdays, name days, New Year, and other occasions

variants.en: Many happy returns! / Happy Birthday! / Happy New Year!

moves.note: neutral; the caller's half of the traditional Orthodox Easter exchange
neutral; the responder's half of the Orthodox Easter exchange, said in reply to 'Hristos a înviat!'
neutral; secular or general Easter wish, not part of the liturgical exchange
neutral; usable across multiple holidays or as a catch-all warm wish
General openers; include both informal (tu) and formal (dumneavoastră) forms
Positive responses to 'how are you?'

variants.en: The fresh air does me good.

moves.note: Negative or middling responses to 'how are you?'

moves.move: Expressing bodily sensations (hunger, thirst, cold, etc.)
moves.note: mi-e + noun constructions; core physical-state vocabulary
Patient reports specific complaints; mă doare (sg.) vs. mă dor (pl.)

variants.ro: Mă doare mai mult la ceafă.
variants.en: It hurts more at the nape of the neck.

variants.ro: Mi-e greață dimineața când mă scol.
variants.en: I feel sick in the morning when I get up.

variants.ro: De 3 zile am o durere de cap continuă.
variants.en: For 3 days I have had a continuous headache.

variants.ro: Acum sufăr de ea în fiecare zi.
variants.en: Now I suffer from it every day.

variants.ro: Am început să tușesc, să fac febră și să mă simt rău.
variants.en: I've started coughing, running a fever and feeling unwell.

moves.note: Formal (dumneavoastră) is standard in medical register; informal forms are informal-register variants

moves.move: Patient asks about seriousness of condition
moves.note: Patient seeks reassurance or clarification from the doctor

variants.ro: Este grav? Trebuie să-mi fac griji?
variants.en: Is it serious? Do I need to worry?
Do I have any problem with my lungs?

moves.note: Formal register throughout; includes reassuring and informative statements

variants.ro: Aveți o tensiune ca la carte.
variants.en: Your blood pressure is perfect / by the book.

variants.ro: Asta arată că aveți o infecție în corp.
variants.en: That shows you have an infection in your body.
No, I think you've caught a cold.

variants.ro: Poate sunteți surmenată. Mergeți la un doctor.
variants.en: Perhaps you are exhausted. Go to a doctor.
Perhaps you are expecting a child.

moves.note: Imperatives used during the physical examination

variants.en: I have to take your blood pressure.

moves.note: Formal; standard medical-encounter moves

variants.ro: Trebuie să faceți analize de sânge.
variants.en: You have to take blood tests.

variants.ro: O să vă dau o trimitere la radiologie.
variants.en: I'll give you a referral to radiology.

moves.note: Formal; includes the prescribing speech act and dosage instructions

variants.en: I will prescribe a course of treatment.

variants.ro: Vă prescriu o rețetă cu câteva medicamente.
variants.en: I'll prescribe you a prescription with a few medications.

variants.ro: Vă prescriu câteva analgezice pentru durerile de cap.
variants.en: I will prescribe you some painkillers for the headaches.

variants.ro: . . . două tablete de trei ori pe zi.
variants.en: . . . two tablets three times a day.

variants.ro: . . . o tabletă numai când vă simțiți rău.
variants.en: . . . one tablet only when you don't feel well.

variants.ro: . . . trei lingurițe de sirop la patru ore.
variants.en: . . . three teaspoons of syrup every four hours.

variants.ro: Vă mai dau și niște vitamine și minerale.
variants.en: I will also give you some vitamins and minerals.

moves.note: Formal imperatives and recommendations for general wellbeing

variants.ro: Trebuie să vă ridicați la fiecare jumătate de oră.
variants.en: You must get up every half hour.

variants.ro: Poate dacă faceți sesiuni regulate de înot, o să vă simțiți mai bine.
variants.en: Perhaps if you swim regularly, you will feel better.

moves.move: Doctor instructs patient to follow up
moves.note: Closing instruction at end of consultation

variants.ro: Dacă nu trec într-o săptămână, vă rog să reveniți.
variants.en: If they don't pass within a week, please come back.

moves.note: Closing move; said by doctor, nurse, or acquaintance

variants.ro: Sper să vă însănătoșiți cât mai repede.
variants.en: I hope you get better as soon as possible.

moves.note: neutral; opens a weather exchange
neutral; E vs. Este are stylistic variants of the same copula

dialogues.scenario: You arrive at the home of your fiancée's parents for the first time. (You use polite dumneavoastră with her mother; she warmly uses tu with you.)

turns.speaker: Tu
turns.ro: Bună ziua, doamnă Ionescu! Mă bucur să vă cunosc.
turns.en: Hello, Mrs Ionescu! I'm glad to meet you.

turns.speaker: Doamna Ionescu
turns.ro: Bună ziua, Tom! Bine ai venit! Poftește înăuntru.

turns.speaker: Tu
turns.ro: Mulțumesc frumos. Aveți o casă foarte frumoasă.
turns.en: Thank you very much. You have a very nice home.

turns.speaker: Doamna Ionescu
turns.ro: Mulțumim! Vrei o cafea sau un ceai?
turns.en: Thank you! Would you like a coffee or a tea?

dialogues.scenario: At the reception, one of the bride's cousins comes over to say hello. (Casual tu throughout.)

turns.speaker: Andrei
turns.ro: Salut! Tu ești Tom, mirele, nu?
turns.en: Hi! You're Tom, the groom, right?

turns.speaker: Tu
turns.en: Thanks a lot! Nice to meet you.

turns.speaker: Andrei
turns.ro: Și mie! Hai să bem ceva.
turns.en: Me too! Let's go get a drink.
